channel.title: Comments for page "Predation and Containment 4975 - What Makes the Cuckoo Tick"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Predation and Containment 4975 - What Makes the Cuckoo Tick"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Article really needs cross-links to its SCP and to 148.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I adore this format and this is an excellent write-up. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree that the SCP chosen limits your options, not a lot of ways to block telekinesis that aren't a little handwavy.

I really like this format - studies are what this wiki needs to be nerd cool again, IMO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, the idea is that the attacks performed by 4975 are telekinetic in nature, and given that telekill's literature has been expanded to show how it halts/weakens telekinesis, it made sense to use it. And to be fair, telekill is about the only way I could have advocated containing it without saying "just let it keep attacking people", which would have serious ethical issues. I also believe it introduces some risk because telekill does have the fact that it releases the energy after a time, meaning that if these procedures are wrong in the long run or they don't completely discharge as expected, then there would still be risks involved. Either way, I tried to do my research, and I believe it would be rather silly of the foundation to not do anything, so I chose the path that keeps in some literary risks and chooses a realistic option.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i actually have the same problem with telekill as fieldstone does with SRAs (not that SRAs would be any better) - it seems like a lazy handwave, and I'm not really sure why blocking telepathy would necessarily do anything. (also, the Foundation would call it SCP-148, rather than telekill.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, I had a few people agree with you on this issue, so I decided to take a new look at it and decided to replace the piece about SRAs with telekill. Please take another look and let me know what you think!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of an article that examines a monster in detail, but this is falling short for me. By far the most important ability 4975 has as a predator is killing people and eating them without actually leaving its cell, which this article lazily sidesteps with SRAs. Failing to truly address that ability undermines the rest of this, because the details of how that long distance attack works seem way more important than the relatively mundane facts about persistence hunting (which mostly describe adaptations that would be made totally obsolete by its telekinesis anyway).

item.title: Re: Rating Module
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I noticed that right after I posted it, it's fixed now though!

item.title: u///u
item.content:encoded: I love this and I'm very flattered that someone would write a tale about an SCP I wrote! It's very in-depth and I'm surprised you managed to make such a strange creature make so much anatomical sense. I really like the concept of this whole academic paper format as well, and I can't wait to see more in this series. +1 from me!

item.title: Rating Module
item.content:encoded: Oi me lad, you forgot to add the rating module.
